title: Worksheet 6: Drawing with Code
description: Learn to create visual patterns by drawing on a grid using coordinates.

problems.title: Your First Pixel
problems.content: Welcome to grid drawing! You can color cells on a grid using the draw() function. The grid uses coordinates like a graph in math class: (x, y). The bottom-left corner is (0, 0). Try running this code to see a single red cell appear!
problems.task: Run the code to draw a red cell at position (1, 1).
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID3)

# Draw a red cell in the center
draw(1, 1, 'red')
problems.hint: Just click 'Run Code' to see the cell appear on the canvas above!

problems.title: Draw a Horizontal Line
problems.content:
Now let's draw multiple cells to create a line. A horizontal line goes from left to right at the same y-coordinate.

The grid is 3×3, so the coordinates go from (0, 0) to (2, 2). Let's draw a black line across the middle row at y=1.
problems.task: Draw a black horizontal line at y=1 by coloring cells (0, 1), (1, 1), and (2, 1).
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID3)

# Draw three cells to make a horizontal line
# draw(x, y) draws black by default
problems.hint: You need three draw() calls: draw(0, 1), draw(1, 1), and draw(2, 1)

problems.title: Color the Corners
problems.content:
Let's make a pattern by coloring the four corner cells of the grid. The corners are at coordinates: (0, 0), (2, 0), (0, 2), and (2, 2).

You can use different colors! Try 'blue', 'green', 'yellow', 'purple', or any color name you know.
problems.task: Draw blue cells in all four corners of the grid.
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID3)

# Color all four corners blue
problems.hint: You need four draw() calls for the corners: (0, 0), (2, 0), (0, 2), and (2, 2)

problems.title: Your First Loop with Drawing
problems.content:
Now let's use a loop to draw! A loop lets us repeat actions without writing the same code over and over.

We'll use GRID100 - a much bigger canvas with 100×100 cells. Instead of drawing 100 cells one by one, we can use a loop!
problems.task: Use a for loop to draw a red horizontal line at y=50 across the entire width of the canvas.
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID100)

# Use a for loop to draw across x from 0 to 99
for x in range(100):
  # Your draw() call here
problems.hint: Inside the loop, use draw(x, 50, 'red') to color each cell

problems.title: Draw a Vertical Line with a Loop
problems.content: Great! Now let's draw a vertical line using a loop. A vertical line has the same x-coordinate but different y-coordinates.
problems.task: Use a for loop to draw a blue vertical line at x=30 from top to bottom.
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID100)

# Use a for loop to draw from y=0 to y=99
problems.hint: Use: for y in range(100): draw(30, y, 'blue')

problems.title: Create a Rainbow
problems.content:
Now for something colorful! We can create a rainbow by drawing horizontal stripes with different colors.

We'll use a list of colors and a loop to draw multiple colored lines.
problems.task: Draw a rainbow with horizontal stripes at y=40, 45, 50, 55, 60, and 65 using the colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple.
problems.starterCode:
use_canvas(GRID100)

colors = ['red', 'orange', 'yellow', 'green', 'blue', 'purple']

# Loop through each color
for i in range(6):
  # Calculate y position: start at 40, add i*5
  y = 40 + i * 5
  
  # Draw a horizontal line at this y position
  for x in range(100):
    # Draw with colors[i]
problems.hint: Inside the inner loop, use draw(x, y, colors[i])

problems.title: Fill a Square
problems.content:
Let's use nested loops to fill in a square! Nested loops let us work with both x and y at the same time.

We'll draw a 20×20 green square in the middle of the canvas.
problems.task: Draw a green square from (40, 40) to (59, 59) using nested loops.
problems.hint: Inside both loops, use draw(x, y, 'green')
